iso_513.source_name: ISO 513:2012 / Machining Doctor ISO Material Groups
iso_513.description_en: ISO 513 classifies workpiece materials for metal cutting with defined cutting edges into six main groups (P/M/K/N/S/H), each with a letter, a conventional color and numbered application subgroups.
iso_513.description_ko: ISO 513은 절삭 가공 대상 피삭재를 P/M/K/N/S/H 6개 주 그룹으로 분류하고, 각 그룹은 고유 문자·색상과 세부 적용 번호(01~50)로 세분화됩니다.

P.description_en: Steel — the largest material group, covering non-alloy, low/medium alloy and high alloy steels, cast steel and free-cutting steel. Long-chipping, moderate cutting forces.
P.description_ko: 강 — 비합금강, 저/중합금강, 고합금강, 주강, 쾌삭강. 긴 칩, 중간 절삭력.
P.application_focus: general turning and milling of steel; long-chip control; good wear resistance required

M.description_en: Stainless steel — austenitic, austenitic-ferritic (duplex), ferritic and martensitic stainless steels plus cast stainless. Work-hardens heavily, sticky chips, high cutting forces.
M.description_ko: 스테인리스강 — 오스테나이트계, 듀플렉스, 페라이트/마르텐사이트, 스테인리스 주강. 가공 경화 심함.
M.application_focus: control of built-up edge and work-hardening; sharp edges, positive geometries, tough grades

K.description_en: Cast iron — grey cast iron (GCI), nodular/ductile iron (SG), malleable iron, compacted graphite iron (CGI). Short-chipping and highly abrasive.
K.description_ko: 주철 — 회주철, 구상흑연주철, 가단주철, CGI. 짧은 칩, 강한 마모성.
K.application_focus: abrasive wear resistance dominant; negative geometry and hard grades

N.description_en: Non-ferrous — aluminum and aluminum alloys, copper, brass, bronze, magnesium, zinc, plus non-metallic materials (plastics, composites). Low cutting forces, very high cutting speeds possible.
N.description_ko: 비철금속 — 알루미늄 합금, 구리/황동/청동, 마그네슘, 아연, 플라스틱/복합재. 낮은 절삭력, 초고속 가공 가능.
N.application_focus: polished rake faces, very sharp edges, fine-grain or PCD grades; chip evacuation

S.description_en: Superalloys and titanium — nickel-based (Inconel, Hastelloy, Waspaloy), cobalt-based and iron-based heat-resistant alloys, plus titanium alloys. High temperature at the edge, severe work-hardening.
S.description_ko: 초내열합금·티타늄 — 니켈기(Inconel 등), 코발트기, 철기 내열합금, 티타늄 합금. 절삭부 고온·가공경화 심함.
S.application_focus: heat-resistant substrates, tough edges, high coolant pressure; low cutting speeds

H.description_en: Hardened materials — hardened steel (typically 48-65 HRC), chilled cast iron, hard-chrome, stellite. Hard turning / hard milling domain.
H.description_ko: 고경도재 — 경화강(48~65 HRC), 칠드 주철, 하드크롬, 스텔라이트. 하드터닝/하드밀링 영역.
H.application_focus: CBN or ultra-hard carbide; negative chamfered edges; rigid setup

subgroups.application_notes: finishing of steel at high cutting speeds, fine surface finish, stable conditions
finishing to light medium cutting of steel, high speeds, light feeds
general-purpose turning and milling of steel, medium speeds and feeds
medium to rough cutting of steel, interrupted cuts, higher feeds
heavy roughing of steel, unstable conditions, heavy interrupted cuts
finishing of stainless steel, high speed, fine finish
general-purpose turning and milling of stainless steel
rough machining of stainless, interrupted cuts, heavy feeds
finishing of grey and nodular cast iron, high abrasion resistance
roughing and interrupted cuts in cast iron
finishing of aluminum and non-ferrous metals, high surface finish
finishing of superalloys and titanium, low speed, stable edge
roughing of superalloys, interrupted cuts, tough edge required
finish hard turning of hardened steel (>55 HRC), CBN territory
general hard machining, light interrupted cuts on hardened workpieces

iso_1832.source_name: ISO 1832:2017 / Carbide Depot insert designation chart
iso_1832.description_en: ISO 1832 defines a 10-position alphanumeric designation for indexable inserts used in turning and milling. Positions 1-7 are mandatory; positions 8-10 are optional (mandatory for PCBN/PCD).
iso_1832.description_ko: ISO 1832은 선삭·밀링용 인덱서블 인서트의 10자리 영숫자 지정 코드를 정의합니다. 1~7자리는 필수, 8~10자리는 선택(초경 CBN/PCD는 필수).

positions.name: shape
positions.meaning: Basic geometric shape of the insert (included angle).

positions.name: clearance_angle
positions.meaning: Normal clearance (relief) angle on the flank of the insert. N = 0° means a negative (double-sided) insert.

positions.name: tolerance
positions.meaning: Tolerance class on inscribed circle (d), thickness (s) and corner-point dimension (m).

example_codes.A: tight tolerance on d, m, s
example_codes.E: close on d and m, medium on s
example_codes.G: ground top and bottom, close on d and s
example_codes.J: close on d, medium on m and s

positions.name: fixing_type
positions.meaning: Type of fixing (hole, countersink) and presence of chipbreakers.

example_codes.G: with hole, chipbreakers on both faces
example_codes.H: with hole, one countersink, chipbreaker on one face
example_codes.J: with hole, two countersinks, chipbreaker on both faces
example_codes.M: with hole, chipbreaker on one face
example_codes.R: no hole, chipbreaker on one face
example_codes.T: with hole, one countersink, chipbreaker on one face
example_codes.U: no hole, chipbreakers on both faces

positions.name: cutting_edge_length
positions.meaning: Nominal cutting-edge length in millimetres (rounded, 1 or 2 digits). For shapes with an inscribed circle (triangle, square, round, rhombic, etc.) it is derived from the IC.

positions.name: thickness
positions.meaning: Insert thickness in millimetres, rounded to two digits (may omit decimals).

positions.name: corner_radius
positions.meaning: Corner (nose) radius in 1/10 mm, two digits. 00 = sharp corner.

positions.name: hand
positions.meaning: Cutting direction / hand of the insert.

positions.name: chipbreaker
positions.meaning: Manufacturer's option field used to encode chipbreaker geometry or grade-specific designation (not rigidly standardized).

vdi_3323.source_name: VDI 3323 — Applicability of hard cutting materials for metal cutting processes
vdi_3323.description: VDI 3323 is a German VDI guideline that complements ISO 513 by defining a finer machinability classification of workpiece materials and a standard way to describe the application suitability of carbide, CBN and PCD grades. Tool makers map their grade recommendations to VDI 3323 classes.

machinability_classes.materials: grey, nodular and malleable cast iron, CGI
aluminum alloys, copper, brass and other non-ferrous
nickel-, cobalt- and iron-based superalloys and titanium alloys
hardened steels and chilled cast iron (hard machining)
